rationale: The skill drives the Wrangler CLI for Cloudflare Workers, so it spawns wrangler (and npx/npm to install or invoke it) and reads/writes wrangler.jsonc, generated TypeScript bindings, .dev.vars, migrations, and the local .wrangler/state directory. It explicitly fetches Cloudflare docs and uses npm to install wrangler, implying network access to developers.cloudflare.com, registry.npmjs.org, and Cloudflare's deploy/auth APIs. Container subcommands invoke docker-style image builds/pushes against Cloudflare's registry (and potentially DockerHub/ECR), and observability/tail commands stream from Cloudflare endpoints. Deletes are largely confined to local dev state, while remote deletions (KV, R2, D1, etc.) happen via the wrangler API rather than filesystem removal.
